arts.ttl: Sheffield United vs West Bromwich Albion – Preview
arts.sum: Sheffield United will be looking to get back to winning ways on Sunday afternoon when they host manager-less West Bromwich Albion. The Blades have produced two poor showings at Bramall Lane of late, managing to beat Plymouth Argyle 2-0 despite conceding plenty of big chances, and more recently losing 2-0 to Burnley on Boxing Day […] The post Sheffield United vs West Bromwich Albion – Preview appeared first on Sheff United Way.

arts.ttl: Carlos Corberan reveals 'intense' episode and confirms West Brom exodus
arts.sum: Carlos Corberan admitted that the last few days over Christmas have been 'intense' as he completed an emotional return to Valencia from West Bromwich Albion. On the day before Christmas Eve, though, Corberan received communication from Valencia - his boyhood club - about the possibility of replacing Ruben Baraja, who had only been sacked earlier that day.

arts.ttl: “We can get back to where we belong” – Carlos Corberan confident of turning things around at Valencia
arts.sum: On Saturday, new Valencia head coach Carlos Corberan was unveiled to the media, having been appointed earlier this week after Los Che triggered the release clause in his West Bromwich Albion release clause. arts.ttl: What West Brom are looking for as Bilkul set about replacing Carlos Corberan
arts.sum: The noises coming out of The Hawthorns suggest that Albion's intentions to find a suitable successor to Corberan won't be rushed simply to ensure a new manager is in place at the earliest opportunity; the hierarchy are eager to make the right call and, in order to do so, are willing to lean on Brunt, Myhill and Abella for the foreseeable future to oversee first-team responsibilities. Bilkul have introduced a more prudent approach to the way Albion work - indeed returning more to what the club proudly used to be able to champion during the days of Dan Ashworth et al - and, acknowledging that managers come and go, they've prepared for this eventuality.

arts.ttl: Sheffield United Lose to Burnley as Wilder Points Out “Head Scratching Decisions” By Referee
arts.sum: Burnley secured a crucial 2-0 win over Sheffield United at Bramall Lane, handing the Blades their first home defeat of the Championship season.
